sources.name: Press Recognition Panel
sources.dataset: Annual Report on the State of Press Regulation

metadata.methodology: Press freedom ranking and score data is drawn from Reporters Without Borders' annual World Press Freedom Index. The index evaluates 180 countries across five indicators: political context, legal framework, economic context, sociocultural context, and safety of journalists. The UK's score is on a 0-100 scale where 100 represents the highest press freedom. Rankings changed methodology in 2022, making pre/post comparisons approximate.
knownIssues: RSF methodology changed significantly in 2022, introducing a new scoring framework — pre-2022 and post-2022 rankings are not directly comparable
The index measures press freedom conditions, not press quality or accuracy
Ownership concentration data is not included in the RSF index but is a significant structural concern for UK media
Online media and independent journalists are not as well captured as traditional press outlets
